title: SSC CGL 2026 Sectional Timing Tier I 2 2 20260522

questions.question: If 5 & 6 = 55, and 9 & 12 = 105, then find the value of 2 & 16.
questions.explanation: Here, the pattern is: 5 + 6 = 11 and 11*5= 55 9 + 12 = 21 and 21*5= 105 Similarly, 2 + 16 = 18 and 18*5= 90

questions.question: In this question, two statements I and II have been given. These statements may be independent causes or effects of independent causes or a common cause. One of the statements maybe the effect of the other statement. Read all statements and select the correct answer. I. Shop X launches a scheme where an individual has to pay monthly installments continuously for 11 months and the 1-month installment will be paid by the firm. Post completion of the scheme period, an individual can purchase gold jewellery of the total amount accumulated in his account. II. The customer count of Shop X increased tremendously, leading to an increase in the cash flow of Shop X.
options: I is the cause and II is its possible effect
II is a cause and I is its possible effect
Both I and II are effects of a common cause
Both I and II are effects of independent causes
questions.explanation: Both statements I and II share a common background. Shop X introduces a scheme in which a customer has to pay 11 consecutive monthly installment payments; the company will cover one month's payment. An individual can use the entire amount accumulated in his account to purchase gold jewelry after the scheme has ended. As a customer gets one installment free, they found it a good deal and invested in it. As a result, Shop X's customer base grew significantly, which in turn enhanced Shop X's cash flow. So, I is the cause and II is its possible effect.

questions.question: How much does one need to subtract from 3_________5 to obtain 4_________7
questions.explanation: Let the number subtracted = x 3/5 - x = 4/7 x = 3/5 - 4/7 x = (21 - 20)/35 x = 1/35

questions.question: Rita's son-in-law Vikram has a sister, Pooja. Pooja's only child is Aarav. How is Aarav related to Rita?
questions.explanation: Rita's son-in-law Vikram has a sister, Pooja. Pooja's only child is Aarav. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p28449/image036.png) Aarav is Rita's son-in-law's sister's child.

questions.question: A number 1286 divided by 15. If the quotient obtained, is divided by 8, it leaves a remainder. Find the remainder.
questions.explanation: 1286 = 15 * 85 + 11 Quotient = 85 Now, 85 = 8 * 10 + 5 Hence, remainder = 5

questions.question: How many such pairs of numbers are there in '0751693842', each of which has as many digits between them as in number series (both forward and backward direction)?
questions.explanation: The given number is 0751693842. Pairs of digits in forward direction: 0 Pairs of digits in backward direction: 0 So, there are 0 such pair.

questions.question: Select the letter-cluster from among the given options that can replace the question mark (?) in the following series: B N T, C O S, ?, E Q Q, F R P
questions.explanation: ABCDEFGHIJKLM 12345678910111213 NOPQRSTUVWXYZ 14151617181920212223242526 B + 1 = C; C + 1 = D; D + 1 = E; E + 1 = F N + 1 = O; O + 1 = P; P + 1 = Q; Q + 1 = R T - 1 = S; S - 1 = R; R - 1 = Q; Q - 1 = P Hence, the missing letter-cluster is DPR.

questions.question: In this type of questions, question is provided with substitutes for various mathematical symbols, followed by a question involving calculation of an expression or choosing the correct/incorrect equation. You have to put the real signs in the given equation and then solve the questions as required. If '^' stands for '÷', '@' stands for '-', '%' stands for '+', '$' stands for 'x', then find the value of: 144 ^ 6 $ 7 % 58 @ 31 = ?

Read the given statement and courses of action carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statement is true, decide which of the given courses of action logically follows for pursuing. Statement: Several commuters report that fake QR-code stickers have been pasted over official QR boards at paid parking lots. People scan and pay, but the money goes to scammers, and the parking receipt is not generated. Course of action I: Remove all suspicious stickers immediately, verify official QR boards, and run a public alert asking people to pay only after matching the payee name; also trace and block reported QR IDs. Course of action II: Shut all paid parking lots for four months to stop QR-related fraud.
options: Both course of action I and II follow
Only course of action I follows
Either course of action I or II follows
Only course of action II follows
questions.explanation: Course of action I directly targets the fraud method and is practical for quick control and prevention. Course of action II is extreme and unrelated to the core fix; closing all parking creates unnecessary disruption. So only course of action I follows.

questions.question: A word has been given in the question, This word CAN be formed by using the letters of the word given in one of the alternatives only. Select that alternative. TIME
questions.explanation: Option (a): JUDGEMENT: 'I' is not in this word. So, option (a) is incorrect. Option (b): HUMIDITY: 'E' is not in this word. So, option (b) is incorrect. Option (c): MIXTURE - M, I, T, E. So, option (c) is correct. Option (d): VITAMIN: 'E' is not in this word. So, option (d) is incorrect. Thus, 'TIME' can be formed using MIXTURE.

questions.question: Select the term that will come next in the following series. 3, 6, 18, 72, 360, __
questions.explanation: Multiply by consecutive integers: 3 X 2 = 6 6 X 3 = 18 18 X 4 = 72 72 X 5 = 360 360 X 6 = 2160

questions.question: Select the letter-number cluster from among the given options that can replace the question mark (?) in the following series. A1Z, C3X, F6U, J10Q, __
questions.explanation: First letters jump by +2, +3, +4, +5: A(1)→C(3)→F(6)→J(10)→O(15). Last letters move backward by −2, −3, −4, −5: Z(26)→X(24)→U(21)→Q(17)→L(12). Numbers: 1, 3, 6, 10 increase by +2, +3, +4; next +5 => 15. Hence O15L.

questions.question: In a code, each letter is replaced by the letter 2 places before it, then the entire result is reversed. How is TOWER coded?
questions.explanation: Each letter is replaced by the letter 2 places before it: T → R O → M W → U E → C R → P So, TOWER becomes RMUCP. Now reverse the result: PCUMR

questions.question: In a certain code language, '+' denotes ' X ', '−' denotes '+', ' X ' denotes '−', and '÷' denotes '÷'. What will be the value of the following expression? 16 + 4 X 5 − 12 ÷ 3.
questions.explanation: Replace symbols as given: + means , X means −, − means +, means . So the expression becomes 16 4 − 5 + 12 3. Applying BODMAS: 16 4 = 64 and 12 3 = 4. Thus, 64 − 5 + 4 = 63.

questions.question: What should come in the place of the question mark (?) in the given series based on the English alphabetical order? AYM, CXN, EWO, GVP, ?, KTR
questions.explanation: In this, AYM CXN EWO GVP IUQ KTR So, option (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the number from among the given options that can replace the question mark (?) in the following series. 7, 9, 13, 21, 37, ?
questions.explanation: Pattern: 7 → 9 (+2) 9 → 13 (+4) 13 → 21 (+8) 21 → 37 (+16) The increments double each time: 2, 4, 8, 16, so next increment = 32. 37 + 32 = 69

questions.question: In the following question, four letter-number pairs are given. The letter-number on the left side of ( - ) is related to the letter-number on the right side of ( - ) with some Logic/Rule/Relation. Three pairs are similar on the basis of same Logic/Rule/Relation. Select the odd one out from the given alternatives. (NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the number into its constituent digits.)

In the given question, there is a certain relationship between two given letter pairs on one side of (::) and a pair that is given on another side of (::) where another pair is to be found from the given alternatives, having the same relationship with this pair as the pairs of the given pair bear. Choose the best alternative. VB : EE :: HK : ?
questions.explanation: We know that, AlphabetsABCDEFGHIJKLM Positional value12345678910111213 AlphabetsZYXWVUTSRQPON Positional value26252423222120191817161514 Here, the logic is: V-E forms the reverse alphabetical pair as per the English alphabetical series. B+3= E Similarly, H-S forms the reverse alphabetical pair as per the English alphabetical series. K+3= N

questions.question: In a certain code language, 'technology improves life' is coded as 'ta@1 mk&8 df=4', 'life needs balance' is coded as 'df=4 lo<7 zx^3', 'technology needs control' is coded as 'lo<7 ta@1 kg$2' and 'control ensures safety' is coded as 'kg$2 paλ5 qw?6'. Which of the statements is true?
questions.explanation: In a certain code language, 'technology improves life' is coded as 'ta@1 mk&8 df=4' 'life needs balance' is coded as 'df=4 lo<7 zx^3' 'technology needs control' is coded as 'lo<7 ta@1 kg$2' 'control ensures safety' is coded as 'kg$2 paλ5 qw?6' The code for 'Technology' = ta@1 The code for 'Life' = df=4 The code for 'Needs' = lo<7 The code for 'Control' = kg$2 The code for 'Ensures' / 'Safety' = paλ5 / qw?6 Thus, the code for 'Improves' = mk&8

questions.question: In a stack of 295 trays, Tray A is 64th from the bottom and Tray B is 98th from the top. How many trays are between Tray A and Tray B?
questions.explanation: In a stack of 295 trays, Tray A is 64th from the bottom and Tray B is 98th from the top. 97 trays Tray B (98th from the top) 'N' Tray A(64th from the bottom) 63 trays N = 295 - [97 + B(1) + A(1) + 63] N = 295 - (97 + 1 + 1 + 63) N = 295 - 162 N = 133

questions.question: By interchanging the given numbers (not digits). Which of the following equations will be correct? 5 and 11 I. 147 + [(51 - 16) ÷ 11] x 5 = 224 II. [(263 + 47) ÷ 5] - 15 x 11 = 27
questions.explanation: If the numbers 5 and 11 (not digits) are interchanged, then the equation will be: Equation I: 147 + [(51 - 16) ÷ 5] x 11 = 224 So, 147 + [35 ÷ 5] x 11 = 224 So, 147 + 7 x 11 = 224 So, 147 + 77 = 224 So, 224 = 224 So, equation I is correct. Equation II: [(263 + 47) ÷ 11] - 15 x 5 = 27 So, [310 ÷ 11] - 15 x 5 = 27 So, 28.18 - 75 = 27 So, -46.82 ≠ 27 So, equation II is incorrect.

questions.question: Select the correct mirror image of the given figure when the mirror is placed at line MN as shown. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26574/image015.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26574/image016.png)
questions.explanation: The correct mirror image is: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26574/image017.png) So, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the given question, two statements are given followed by three conclusions. You have to take the given statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance from commonly known facts. Read the conclusions and decide which of the given conclusions logically follows from the given statements disregarding commonly known facts. Statements: Some connects are networks. No network is a project. Conclusions: I. No project is a connect. II. Some networks are connects. III. All projects are connects.
questions.explanation: Some connects are networks. No network is a project. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p7933/image004.png) Some connects may or may not be projects. So, conclusions I and III do not follow. Some networks are connects is true. So, only conclusion II follows.

questions.question: Select the option which is related to the fifth term in the same way as the second term is related to the first term and the fourth term is related to the third term. AFTER : ZJWKT : : MODEL : LSGKN : : LIGHT : ?
questions.explanation: AFTER -- A Z, F (GHI) J, T (UV) W, E (FGHIJ) K, R (S) T MODEL -- M L, O (PQR) S, D (EF) G, E (FGHIJ) K, L (M) N LIGHT -- L K, I (JKL) M, G (HI) J, H (IJKLM) N, T (U) V

questions.question: Introducing Meena to Raman, Kapil said, "She is the daughter of my mother's only daughter's son". How is Kapil related to Meena's father?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p23675/image021.png) So, Kapil is the uncle of Meena's father.

questions.question: The Adani Group recently signed a Power Purchase and Concession Agreement for a 570 MW hydropower project involving an investment of ₹6,000 crore. In which of the following countries is this project being developed?
questions.explanation: 570 MW hydropower project in Bhutan by Adani Power and Druk Green Power. Investment: ₹6,000 crore. Model: BOOT (Build, Own, Operate, Transfer). Agreement signed: Power Purchase andConcession Agreement. Attendees: Bhutanese Prime Minister DashoTshering Tobgay and Adani Group ChairmanGautam Adani.

questions.question: In the traditional Garba dance, a 'Garbha Deep' (lamp placed inside a clay pot) is used as a symbolic element. What does this lamp primarily represent?
questions.explanation: The Garbha Deep symbolizes life, creation, and the divine feminine energy. The lamp placed inside the clay pot represents a living soul glowing inside the mother's womb - a central theme of Garba. Further Insights Garba is performed during Navratri to honor Goddess Durga. The clay pot used in Garba is called Garbha / Garbi / Garbha Deep. Garba represents the cycle of birth, life, and death. The circular movements symbolize the cyclic nature of time in Hindu philosophy. Garba originates from Gujarat and is one of India's most famous folk dances.

questions.question: Given below are two statements, one labelled as Assertion (A) and the other as Reason (R): Assertion (A): The Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA) guarantees at least 100 days of wage employment in a financial year to every rural household. Reason (R): Under the Act, the State Governments are legally bound to provide employment within 15 days of application, failing which unemployment allowance must be paid. Which of the following is correct?
options: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is true, but R is false.
A is false, but R is true.
questions.explanation: Assertion (A) is true: MGNREGA is a rights-based employment guarantee law that assures at least 100 days of unskilled wage employment in a financial year to every rural household whose adult members volunteer to do unskilled manual work. This guarantee is clearly stated in the Act itself. Reason (R) is true: The Act mandates that employment must be provided within 15 days of application. If the State fails to do so, it must pay an unemployment allowance to the applicant. This provision makes the scheme legally enforceable. Why R is NOT the correct explanation of A The 100-day employment guarantee (Assertion) is a core entitlement under the Act. The 15-day provision and unemployment allowance (Reason) are implementation mechanisms designed to ensure accountability and timely delivery of employment. While R strengthens enforcement of the scheme, it does not explain why the guarantee is fixed at 100 days. Hence, both statements are correct, but R does not directly explain A.

questions.question: The Master Control Facility (MCF) of ISRO, located in Hassan (Karnataka) and Bhopal (Madhya Pradesh), is primarily responsible for which of the following tasks?
options: Launching of Polar Satellite Launch Vehicles (PSLV)
Post-launch monitoring and control of Geostationary satellites
Designing of remote sensing camera payloads
questions.explanation: ISRO's Master Control Facility (MCF) is responsible for Tracking Orbit raising Health monitoring Commanding and controlling of geostationary (GEO) and geosynchronous satellites (GSO) like INSAT, GSAT, and NavIC. Cryogenic engines are developed and tested at LPSC (Liquid Propulsion Systems Centre) Launching of PSLV (and other rockets) happens at Satish Dhawan Space Centre (SDSC), Sriharikota. Design of imaging payloads is done at Space Applications Centre (SAC), Ahmedabad Further Insights MCF controls satellites like INSAT, GSAT, KALPANA, IRNSS/NavIC. It handles telemetry, tracking, and command (TT&C) operations. GEO satellites orbit at 36,000 km altitude. MCF performs orbit-raising operations after satellite injection. Satellite life-extension maneuvers are planned and executed from MCF.

questions.question: What natural phenomenon did the Harappan engineers exploit to allow large vessels to enter the dockyard basin?
questions.explanation: The Harappans used tidal action to operate the Lothal dockyard. The dockyard at Lothal (Gujarat) was connected to the sea. Harappan engineers relied on high tides to raise water levels. This allowed large boats to enter the dock basin safely. At low tide, the water receded, enabling controlled movement of vessels and preventing silt blockage. Further Insights Lothal's dockyard is considered one of the oldest known dockyards in the world. The Harappans had advanced knowledge of hydrology and maritime engineering. Lothal was a major centre for bead-making and overseas trade. A spillway system ensured excess water was drained safely. The dockyard was connected to the Sabarmati River channel, which once flowed closer to the site.

questions.question: India has created underground Strategic Petroleum Reserve (SPR) facilities for emergency oil storage. At which of the following places is an SPR not located?
questions.explanation: Kochi has important energy infrastructure such as an LNG terminal, port facilities, and refineries. But the official SPR program includes only three underground rock-cavern reserves: Visakhapatnam Mangaluru Padur Visakhapatnam in Andhra Pradesh has one of India's first SPR sites,It is an underground rock cavern with large storage capacity. Mangaluru (Karnataka) hosts a major SPR facility built inside deep underground caverns. Padur (near Udupi, Karnataka) is another key SPR location. Further Insights India's SPR program is managed by Indian Strategic Petroleum Reserves Ltd. (ISPRL). The combined storage capacity is around 5.33 million tonnes, A Phase - II expansion is planned at Chandikhol (Odisha) and Padur (Karnataka). SPRs protect India during global oil shocks or supply disruptions.

questions.question: The Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS), 2023 - which replaced the Indian Penal Code (IPC), 1860 - was mainly drafted on the recommendations of which committee?
options: Malimath Committee on Reforms of Criminal Justice System
National Committee for Reforms in Criminal Laws (Ranbir Singh Committee)
J.S. Verma Committee on Amendments to Criminal Law
questions.explanation: The committee was established in 2020 by the Ministry of Home Affairs. It was tasked with recommending changes to the IPC, CrPC, and Indian Evidence Act. Malimath Committee This committee (2000 - 03) suggested reforms in the criminal justice system, focusing on victim rights, standard of proof, etc. Its main concern was criminal trials Law Commission of India (22nd Commission) The 22nd Law Commission has worked on various issues (sedition, simultaneous elections, etc.). J.S. Verma Committee Formed after the 2012 Delhi gang rape case. Gave recommendations on sexual offences, women's safety, and punishment reforms. Further Insights BNS replaces the 163-year-old IPC (1860) with modernized crime definitions. The term "sedition" was removed and replaced with a new offence dealing with acts endangering sovereignty. BNS introduces new crimes like organized crime, mob lynching, and terrorism. Community service has been introduced as a form of punishment for minor offences. The new criminal law aims to make procedures faster, technology-based, and victim-friendly.

questions.question: With reference to the Baul tradition of Bengal, consider the following statements: 1) It is a mystical philosophy and music genre that emphasizes the search for the "Maner Manush" (the inner soul) while transcending formal religious boundaries. 2) The musical performances are traditionally characterized by the use of instruments such as the Ektara and the Dubki. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The Baul tradition of Bengal is not just a musical style but a spiritual-philosophical movement. Bauls seek the "Maner Manush" or "the man of the heart," symbolizing the inner divine or the true self. They reject rigid religious identities - Hindu, Muslim, or otherwise - and promote a humanistic, experiential spiritual path rooted in love, simplicity, and inner exploration. Baul music is known for its simple yet soulful instruments. Two of the primary traditional instruments used are: Ektara - A single-string plucked instrument, symbolic of the unity of God. Dubki - A small percussion instrument (similar to a hand drum) used to maintain rhythm. Further Insights The Baul tradition is recognized by UNESCO as an Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity (2008). Baul songs often emphasize love, humanity, and internal spiritual realization. Bauls traditionally roam from village to village singing for food or alms. Their music blends elements of Vaishnavism, Sufism, and Tantric traditions.

questions.question: Which specific organization's anniversary was commemorated with a special coin and a 'Chhota Bheem' comic book in New Delhi in November 2025?
questions.explanation: The National Hydroelectric Power Corporation (NHPC) celebrated its 50th anniversary, and to mark this milestone, a special commemorative coin and a 'Chhota Bheem' comic book themed on clean energy were released in New Delhi. This initiative aimed to spread awareness about hydropower and renewable energy among children. Further Insights NHPC is India's premier hydropower development company established in 1975. India has significant hydropower potential due to its Himalayan river systems. Hydropower contributes to grid stability as it can be started and stopped quickly. The government is promoting renewable energy to achieve net-zero targets by 2070.

questions.question: Who among the following took over as the President of the Federation of Indian Chambers of Commerce & Industry (FICCI) for the 2025 - 2026 term at its 98th Annual General Meeting?
questions.explanation: Anant Goenka took over as the President of FICCI for the 2025 - 26 term at its 98th Annual General Meeting. He succeeded Harsha Vardhan Agarwal in this role. Further Insights FICCI is one of India's oldest and largest industry associations, established in 1927. The President of FICCI plays a key role in industry policy advocacy and government engagement. FICCI's leadership is refreshed annually at its Annual General Meeting. The organization partners with industry and government to promote economic growth and reforms. FICCI's Director General is Jyoti Vij.

questions.question: Given below are two statements, one labelled as Assertion (A) and the other as Reason (R): Assertion (A): The Governor of a State can allow the Union Government (or its officers) to carry out certain State-level executive functions, but only with the consent of the Government of India. Reason (R): Article 258A was added by the 7th Constitutional Amendment Act, 1956, to let States formally hand over certain administrative functions to the Centre. In the context of the above statements, which of the following is correct?
options: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Assertion (A) - True A Governor can allow the Central Government to perform certain State-level executive tasks, but only if the Government of India agrees. This is directly allowed under Article 258A of the Constitution. Reason (R) - True Article 258A was indeed added by the 7th Constitutional Amendment, 1956. Its purpose was to create a two-way system in which: the Centre can give its functions to States (Article 258), and States can give their functions to the Centre (Article 258A). Why R correctly explains A The Assertion says the Governor can give some State functions to the Centre with the Centre's consent. The Reason explains the exact constitutional article and the amendment that created this power. So, R perfectly explains why A is true. Further Insights Article 258 allows the Centre to delegate tasks to States without amending the Constitution. Administrative cooperation between Centre and States is a key feature of India's quasi-federal system.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: According to Census 2011, Buddhists have a literacy rate of _________, higher than the national average of 72.98%.
questions.explanation: As per Census 2011, Buddhists have a literacy rate of 81.29%, higher than the national average of 72.98%, according to Census data. The literacy rate among Hindus is 73.27% while Scheduled Castes have a lower literacy rate of 66.07%. Further insights: There are more than 8.4 million Buddhists in India and 87% of them are converts from other religions, mostly Dalits who changed religion to escape Hindu caste oppression. The remaining 13% of Buddhists belong to traditional communities of the northeast and northern Himalayan regions. Today, these converts to Buddhism, also called neo-Buddhists, enjoy better literacy rates, greater work participation and sex ratio than Scheduled Caste Hindus, the group from which most converts emerge, according to an IndiaSpend analysis of 2011 Census data.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the "Mopin" festival: 1)The 'Mopin' or 'Moopin' Festival is an agricultural festival celebrated by the Galo tribe of Arunachal Pradesh. 2) The Mopin Festival is celebrated in the month April from 4th to 6th of every year. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The 'Mopin' or 'Moopin' Festival is an agricultural festival celebrated by the Galo tribe of Arunachal Pradesh, in particular of the Galo group of tribes which resides in East Siang and West Siang district. The Mopin Festival is celebrated in the month April from 4th to 6th of every year. On this occasion worships and chant the divine words of praise to mother goddess, Digo-Ane for continual protection from epidemic drought, famine. On this day all the women folk perform Anyi Pingku and Popir dance and all the men perform Digo-Tudi to appease the gods and goddess Ane Pingku and Pintu for peace, prosperity and happiness.

questions.question: With reference to the fundamental elements of a Raga in Indian Classical Music, consider the following statements: 1) Avroh refers to the ascending sequence of notes, while Aaroh represents the descending sequence of notes in a Raga. 2) The Vadi is the primary, most frequently used note (the "King"), whereas the Samvadi is the secondary important note (the "Minister"). Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Vadi = Most important note The Vadi is the dominant, most emphasized note of the raga. It is often compared to a King because it is the central note around which the raga's character revolves. Samvadi = Second-most important note The Samvadi supports the Vadi like a "Minister," giving balance and stability to the raga. Aaroh = Ascending Avroh = Descending Aaroh (Arohana): Notes go upwards Avroh (Avarohana): Notes go downwards Further Insights A raga is built from selected notes (swaras) following specific rules. The time of performance (prahar) can significantly affect a raga's emotional effect. Some ragas use all seven notes (sampoorna), while others use five or six. The Pakad is the signature phrase that helps identify a raga instantly. Ragas evoke specific moods - called rasa, such as devotion, romance, or peace.

questions.question: In the L. Chandra Kumar v. Union of India (1997) case, the Supreme Court held that the power of judicial review forms part of the Basic Structure of the Constitution. These powers are primarily derived from which Articles?
questions.explanation: In L. Chandra Kumar (1997), the Supreme Court ruled that judicial review by High Courts and the Supreme Court forms part of the Basic Structure of the Constitution. These powers come from: Article 32 → Supreme Court writ jurisdiction Article 226 → High Court writ jurisdiction Further Insights The Basic Structure doctrine was first established in Kesavananda Bharati (1973). The judgment ensured that tribunals cannot replace High Courts for judicial review. Judicial review allows courts to strike down unconstitutional laws. Dr. B.R. Ambedkar called Article 32 the "heart and soul" of the Constitution.

questions.question: Indian-born historian Sunil Amrith won the 2025 British Academy Book Prize for his book "The Burning Earth." The book primarily focuses on which theme?
questions.explanation: Sunil Amrith's book "The Burning Earth" examines how climate change, environmental degradation, and ecological shifts have shaped human history and continue to influence societies. It focuses on environmental history, showing how ecological transformations impact human lives, migration, and development Further insights Indian-born historian Sunil Amrith Work: The Burning Earth: An Environmental History of the Last 500 Years Prize: 2025 British Academy Book Prize Prize amount: ≤25,000 Purpose: To honor outstanding non-fictionbooks from around the world

questions.question: Consider the following statements: 1) ASEAN is a regional organization comprising Southeast Asian nations focused on political, economic, and security cooperation. 2) Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced that the year 2026 will be celebrated as the "ASEAN - Asian Countries Year of Maritime Cooperation." Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: ASEAN (Association of Southeast Asian Nations) includes 10 countries from Southeast Asia and works on improving cooperation in political stability, economic growth, social development, security coordination, and cultural exchange. PM Modi announced 2026 will be celebrated as the "ASEAN - India Year of Maritime Cooperation." Not "ASEAN - Asian Countries Year of Maritime Cooperation." Further Insights ASEAN was founded on 8 August 1967 with 5 original members. India became a Sectoral Partner of ASEAN in 1992 and a Strategic Partner in 2012. ASEAN's headquarters is located in Jakarta, Indonesia. India and ASEAN conduct the SIMBEX naval exercise with Singapore and maritime drills with other ASEAN nations. The ASEAN-India cooperation focuses heavily on the Indo-Pacific region and secure sea routes.

questions.question: Fatehpur Sikri, built by Emperor Akbar, is famous for its distinct architectural style. Which material was mainly used to construct most of its buildings?
questions.explanation: Fatehpur Sikri's architecture is dominated by red sandstone, giving the city its iconic reddish look. Almost all palaces, courtyards, halls, mosques, and administrative buildings were constructed using locally available red sandstone. This material was easy to carve, durable, and suitable for Akbar's blend of Persian, Timurid, and Indian styles. Further Insights Fatehpur Sikri was Akbar's capital from 1571 to 1585. The Buland Darwaza in the city is one of the tallest gateways in the world. The architectural style blends Persian, Islamic, and local Hindu elements.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: 1) When an object is at a height, it has maximum kinetic energy; as it falls, that energy "turns into" potential energy as its speed increases. 2) A swinging pendulum is a classic example. At the highest points of its swing, it has 100% kinetic energy; at the lowest point (the center), it has 100% potential energy. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: When an object is at a height, it has maximum potential energy, not kinetic energy. As it falls, potential energy (PE) decreases, and kinetic energy (KE) increases. Energy transforms from potential → kinetic, not the other way around. The energy distribution in a pendulum is the opposite of what is stated: At the highest points → speed is zero → 100% potential energy, 0% kinetic energy. At the lowest point (center) → speed is maximum → maximum kinetic energy, minimum potential energy. Further Insights Energy cannot be created or destroyed; it only changes form (Law of Conservation of Energy). A freely falling body converts gravitational potential energy entirely into kinetic energy (neglecting air resistance). In real pendulums, friction and air resistance slowly reduce total mechanical energy. At the midpoint of a pendulum, velocity is maximum; acceleration is zero. Potential energy depends on height; kinetic energy depends on speed.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about Dhinidhi Desinghu at the 2025 Asian Youth Games: 1) Dhinidhi Desinghu set a new national record in the Women's 200-meter backstroke. 2) The record-breaking performance took place in Abu Dhabi, with a timing of approximately 4 minutes 21 seconds. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Dhinidhi Desinghu set a new national record in the 400-metre freestyle, not the 200-metre backstroke, at the 2025 Asian Youth Games The performance did not happen in Abu Dhabi. The Asian Youth Games 2025 took place in Bahrain National Record in Women's 400m Freestyle Athlete: DinendiDesindhu Event: 2025 Asian Youth Games, Bahrain Category: Women's 400-meter freestyle Record Time: Approximately 4 minutes 21 seconds.

questions.question: The Western Ghats rise steeply along the western side of India. This height mainly causes which type of rainfall?
questions.explanation: Orographic rainfall occurs when moist winds hit a mountain barrier and rise up the slope. The Western Ghats stand tall along the west coast. Moist monsoon winds from the Arabian Sea are forced to rise along these mountains. As the air rises, it cools and causes heavy rainfall on the windward side. Cyclonic rainfall happens due to low-pressure systems like tropical cyclones or depressions Convectional rainfall occurs due to intense heating of land, leading to rising hot air and afternoon showers. Retreating monsoon occurs during October - November, caused by shifting winds and cyclonic systems. Further Insights The Western Ghats create a powerful rain-shadow region on their eastern side (e.g., Bengaluru, Pune). They are responsible for one of the wettest places on Earth - Mawsynram lies in a similar orographic zone in the east. Orographic rainfall is most intense during the Southwest Monsoon (June - September). The height of the Ghats ranges from 900 to 1600 metres, creating a strong barrier. Coastal Karnataka and Kerala receive heavy monsoon rainfall due to the Ghats' uplift effect.

questions.question: For which of the following novels did Kiran Desai receive the Man Booker Prize in 2006?
questions.explanation: The Inheritance of Loss won the Man Booker Prize in 2006, making Kiran Desai the youngest woman ever to receive the award at that time. The novel deals with themes of migration, colonial legacy, identity conflict, and social inequality set in Kalimpong and New York. Hullabaloo in the Guava Orchard Written by Kiran Desai, but not the Booker Prize-winning book. It is her debut novel published in 1998. The God of Small Things Written by Arundhati Roy, Won the Booker Prize in 1997, A Fine Balance Written by Rohinton Mistry, Shortlisted for the Booker in 1996 but did not win.

questions.question: Which North Indian ruler was defeated by Rajendra I during his military expedition to the banks of the Ganges?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I, the Chola king, launched a northern expedition (Gangaikonda expedition) to assert Chola power across India. During this campaign, he defeated Mahipala I, the Pala ruler of Bengal. This victory is commemorated in his title "Gangaikonda Chola" (the Chola who conquered the Ganga). Further Insights Rajendra I was the son of Rajaraja I, one of the greatest Chola rulers. Rajendra I's northern campaign extended Chola influence up to the Ganges River. After victory, he founded a new capital GangaikondaCholapuram. The Cholas were known for their strong naval power, trade, and maritime influence. The Pala dynasty was a major Buddhist power in eastern India, known for supporting Nalanda and Vikramashila universities.

questions.question: Regarding the structure of the human ear, which of the following is NOT a component involved in phonoreception (hearing)?
questions.explanation: Papillae are tiny projections found on the tongue, containing taste buds. They are part of the gustatory system (taste) Pinna It is part of the outer ear and collects sound waves. Cochlea It is a part of the inner ear, responsible for converting sound vibrations into nerve signals. Malleus It is one of the three ossicles (ear bones) in the middle ear involved in transmitting sound vibrations. Further Insights The three middle-ear bones are malleus, incus, stapes, and are the smallest bones in the human body. The Eustachian tube maintains pressure balance in the ear. Vibrations travel from pinna → ear canal → eardrum → ossicles → cochlea → brain.

questions.question: With reference to the integration of Princely States in post-independence India, consider the following statements: 1) The "States Department" was established in 1947 specifically to handle matters related to the relationship between the Central Government and the Princely States. 2) The department was headed by Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel, with G.V. Mavalankar serving as the Administrative Secretary. 3) The "Carrot and Stick" policy used by the department involved offering privy purses and autonomy as incentives while maintaining the threat of military or political consequences for resistance. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The States Department (later renamed Ministry of States) was established in 1947 under the Government of India to manage all negotiations and administrative matters with Princely States. It coordinated their accession, merger, and integration into the Indian Union. The Patel - Menon team indeed used a "Carrot and Stick" approach, offering: Carrot: Privy purses, guarantees of autonomy, titles, dignities. Stick: Pressure through public opinion, political tactics, and if needed, military action (e.g., police action in Hyderabad). The States Department was headed by Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel, but the secretary was V.P. Menon, not G.V. Mavalankar. Mavalankar was the first Speaker of the Lok Sabha, not a secretary of the States Department. Further Insights India had 565 princely states at the time of independence. The Instrument of Accession (IoA) allowed states to join India on three subjects - defence, foreign affairs, and communications. The integration process included accession, merger agreements, and reorganisation of states. Hyderabad required military intervention under Operation Polo in 1948. Junagadh was integrated after a public plebiscite in 1948.

questions.question: A grocer mixes 12 kg of tea at Rs. 30/kg and 18 kg of tea at Rs. 40/kg. if he sold the mixture at Rs. 52/kg. find his profit percentage.
questions.explanation: Total cost price for the grocer = 12 * 30 + 18 * 40 = Rs. 1080 Total selling price = 52 * (12 + 18) = 52 * 30 = Rs. 1560 Amount of profit = 1560 - 1080 = Rs. 480 Required percentage = 480/1080 * 100 = 44 (4/9)%

questions.question: The non-terminating repeating decimal among the following fractions is:
questions.explanation: [If the denominator contains only factors of 2 and/or 5, then its decimal expansion will terminate] (a) 153/4 Factor of 4 = 2 * 2 [Terminating decimal] (b) 189/20 Factor of 20 = 2 * 2 * 5 [Terminating decimal] (c) 147/26 Factor of 26 = 2 * 13 [non-terminating decimal] (d) 177/8 Factor off 8 = 2 * 2 * 2 [Terminating decimal]

questions.question: A number is multiplied by 5_________8 instead of 2_________5 . What is the percentage error in the calculation?
questions.explanation: Let the number be x. Wrong value = 5x/8 Corrected value = 2x/5 Hence, the required percentage = [(5x/8 - 2x/5) * 100]/ (2x/5) = (9x/40 * 100)/ (2x/5) = 56.25%

questions.question: A, B, and C together start a business. Two times the investment of A equals three times the Investment of B and the capital of B is four times that of C. Find the respective ratio of shares of A, B and C in the profit.
questions.explanation: I = Investment T = Time Let the investment be C be 'X' B's investment = 4X 2A's investment = 3B's investment 2A's investment = 3 * 4X A's investment = 6X Hence, the required ratio = 6: 4: 1

questions.question: If A's salary is 20% less than that of B, then how much percent is B's salary more than that of A?
questions.explanation: Let salary of B = Rs.100 Then, salary of A = 100 x 80/100 = Rs.80 Required percentage = (20 x 100/80) = 25%

questions.question: The total surface area of a right circular cylinder is 54π cm2. If its height is twice its radius, what is its volume?
questions.explanation: Let the radius of a cylinder be r cm height = 2r Total surface area = 54π 2πr(h + r) = 54π r (2r + r) = 27 r² = 9 r = 3 cm h = 6 cm Hence, volume of cylinder = πr²h = π * 32 * 6 = 54π cm3 Express Method Volume = π r2 x h = π 2r3 [as h = 2r] = 2π r3 That means if we divide the options by 2π, we will get a perfect cube. And from the given options, only option a satisfies this condition.

questions.question: Three circular fountains are installed in a park, one inside another. The diameters of the fountains are in arithmetic progression. If the circumference of the smallest fountain is 12π m and that of the largest fountain is 20π m, then what is the circumference of the middle fountain?
questions.explanation: The circumference of the smallest fountain is 12π m, so its diameter is 12 m. The circumference of the largest fountain is 20π m, so its diameter is 20 m. The diameters are in arithmetic progression. Therefore, the diameter of the middle fountain is the average of the smallest and largest diameters. Middle diameter = (12 + 20)/2 = 16 m Now, circumference of the middle fountain = π x 16 = 16π m

questions.question: The average of the first eight multiples of 16 is:
questions.explanation: Average of the first eight multiples of 16 = (16*1 + 16*8)/2 = 144/2 = 72

questions.question: A person bought an article for Rs. 5200 and spent Rs. 500 to paint it. Find the profit percentage of the person if he sells the article for Rs. 7125.
questions.explanation: Total cost price for the person = 5200 + 500 = Rs. 5700 Selling price of the article = Rs. 7125 Profit percentage = (7125 - 5700)/5700 * 100 = 25%

questions.question: Given ratios A : B = 3 : 5, B : C = 4 : 7 and C : D = 7 : 12, if D = 240, find the value of A.

Last month, Anju's monthly saving was Rs. 2500 and Varun's monthly saving was Rs. 3200. This month, Anju's monthly saving is Rs 2900. and Varun's monthly saving is Rs. 3800. Find the value of [(X - Y) * 100/Y)], where X% is the percentage increase in Varun's monthly saving this month over last month and Y% is the percentage increase in Anju's monthly saving this month over last month.
questions.explanation: Anju's monthly saving last month = Rs. 2500 Anju's monthly saving this month = Rs. 2900 Y = (2900 - 2500) * 100/2500 = 16% Varun's monthly saving last month = Rs. 3200 Varun's monthly saving this month = Rs. 3800 X = (3800 - 3200) * 100/3200 = 18.75 Hence, [(X - Y) * 100/Y] = [(18.75 - 16) * 100/16] = 17.1875

questions.question: A chord of the larger among two concentric circles is of length 30 cm and it is tangent to the smaller circle. What is the area (in sq. cm) of the annular portion between the two circles?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26626/image014.png) Let Radius of larger circle be R cm and that of smaller circle be r cm. Here AB = 30/2 = 15 cm Area of Annular portion = Area of larger circle - Area of smaller circle = π R2 - π r2 = π (R2 - r2) ....(1) In triangle AOB, R2 = 152 + r2 R2 - r2 = 225 Now, eq (1), Area of annular portion = 225 π cm2

questions.question: A shopkeeper offers a discount of 25% on the marked price and makes a loss of 10%. The marked price is Rs. 1200. If he sells at a discount of 12% on the marked price, then what profit/loss will he make?

A shop had planned to allow a discount of d% on the marked price. At that discount the profit on cost would have been 15%. However, when the discount was increased by 5 percentage points, the profit reduced to 5%. Find the percentage by which the marked price exceeds the cost price.
A boy 2.1 m tall can just see the sun over a 42.8 m tall building, which is 40.7√3 m away from him. The angle of elevation of the sun is:
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p29911/image002.png) BD = CE = Height of boy = 2.1 m AE = Height of building = 42.8 m DE = BC = Distance between boy and building = 40.7√3 m AC = 42.8 m - 2.1 m = 40.7 m Tan θ = 40.7/40.7√3 Tan θ = 1/√3 θ = 30˚

questions.question: Each edge of a right prism measures 12 m and its height is 20 m. If the base is a regular hexagon, what is the volume of the prism?
questions.explanation: Side a = 12 m and height h = 20 m Area of regular hexagon = (3√3/2)a2 = (3√3/2) * 144 = 216√3 m2 Hence, Volume = base area X height = 216√3 X 20 = 4320√3 m3

questions.question: If the value of 1_________32.12 is 0.03113, then what is the value of 1_________0.00003212
A circular metal disc of radius 9 cm has a sector of 120˚ removed. The remaining part is rolled to form a closed cone. What is the volume of the cone (in terms of π, cm3)?
questions.explanation: Original disc radius, R = 9 cm Central angle of remaining sector = 360˚ − 120˚ = 240˚ Arc length of remaining = (240/360) X 2πR = 12π cm Base radius of cone, r = arc length/(2π) = 12π/(2π) = 6 cm Slant height of cone, l = R = 9 cm Height of cone, h = √(l2 - r2) = √(92 - 62) = √(81 − 36) = 3√5 cm Hence, volume of cone = 1/3 X πr2h = 1/3 X π X 62 X 3√5 = 36√5 π cm3

questions.question: The table shows annual revenue (in crores ₹) of a restaurant chain: YearRevenue 201520 201625 201730 201828 201940 202045 Which year had the highest increase compared to the previous year?
The average of 12 items is 56. What single number must be added as the 13th item so that the average becomes 70?
questions.explanation: Required value = (target average X new count) − (current average X current count) Required value = 70 X 13 − 56 X 12 = 910 − 672 = 238

questions.question: A and B invest capitals of ₹120000 and ₹180000 respectively for one year. The annual profit is ₹102000. What is A's share (in ₹)?
questions.explanation: Ratio of profit share of A & B = 120000 : 180000 = 2 : 3 A's share = 102000 X 2 ÷ 5 = ₹40800

questions.question: A chef mixes two types of flour, one costing ₹180/kg and the other ₹220/kg, with a third type of flour. The ratio of the three types in the mix is 2:3:4. If the price of the mixture is ₹240/kg, find the price of the third type of flour.
questions.explanation: Let the price of the third type of flour be Rs. A. According to the question, 180 x 2 + 220 x 3 + 4 x A = (2 + 3 + 4) x 240 360 + 660 + 4A = 2160 4A = 1140 A = Rs.285

questions.question: A principal P amounts to Rs.1375 in 2 years and to Rs.1650 in 4 years at simple interest. Find P.
questions.explanation: Simple interest for 2 years = Rs.1650 - Rs.1375 = Rs.275 Amounts in 2 years = Rs.1375 Principal = Amount - Simple interest = 1375 - 275 = Rs.1100

questions.question: Shilpa invests Rs 12800 at simple interest at 1.5% per annum. After t years, the amount is Rs 13568. Determine t.
The area of a right‑angled triangle is 126 cm². If the length of the base is 14 cm, find the length of the height.
questions.explanation: Area of right-angled triangle = (1/2) X base X height 126 = (1/2) X 14 X height 126 = 7 X height height = 126 / 7 = 18 cm

questions.question: Identify the option that arranges the given parts in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent sentence. A. A key issue in B. Its impact on human society C. The ethical implications of D. The book is E. Artificial intelligence and
questions.explanation: The correct order to form a meaningful and coherent sentence is ADCEB. Part A indicates that there is an issue with something. Part D comes next, as it identifies where the issue lies- in the book. Accordingly, AD forms a logical pair. Part C then specifies the issue. It suggests that the issue is related to the ethical implications of something. Parts E and B then elaborate on the issue and specify what the ethical implications are related to - artificial intelligence. Hence, (b) is the correct answer. The complete sentence is: A key issue in the book is the ethical implications of artificial intelligence and its impact on human society.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct speech. The guests told the manager that they had been enjoying the stay.
options: The guests said to the manager, "We are enjoying the stay".
The guest says to the manager, "We will be enjoying the stay".
The guests said the manager, "We might enjoy our stay".
The guests said to the manager, "We were enjoying the stay".
questions.explanation: The sentence in the Indirect Speech is in Past Perfect Continuous Tense. Therefore, the sentence in the Direct Speech will be in Past Continuous Tense. Past Perfect Continuous (Subject + had been + V1 + ing + Object) will be converted into Past Continuous (Subject + was/were+V1 + ing + Object). For example: Jack said that he had been going through a rough phase. (Indirect Speech) Jack said, "I was going through a rough phase". (Direct Speech) The following things in the sentence will change when changed from Indirect speech to Direct Speech:Indirect SpeechDirect Speech told... thatsaid to theyWe have been enjoyingwere enjoying The sentence that follows the above-mentioned conditions is option (d) "The guests said to the manager, "We were enjoying the stay"." Hence, the correct answer is option (d).

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. The audit team completed the internal verification of financial documents last week.
options: The internal verification of financial documents was completed by the audit team last week.
The internal verification of financial documents has been completed by the audit team last week.
The internal verification of financial documents is completed by the audit team last week.
The audit team was completed by the internal verification of financial documents last week.
questions.explanation: The subject of the Active Voice is changed into the object of the Passive Voice. Also, the object of the Active Voice is changed into the subject of the Passive Voice. The usage of a "to be" form of verb ["was" in this case] is necessary while changing the voice of the sentence from Active to Passive. In Passive Voice, the preposition "by" is used before the object. However, it is optional in some cases. The Active voice in this question is in Past Indefinite, indicated by the verb "completed". Thus, the Passive voice will also be in Past Indefinite. The format of the Active voice is: Subject + V2 + Object. Thus, in Passive voice, the format will be: Object of the active voice (Subjective case) + was/were + V3 + by + Subject of the active voice (Objective case). For example: Active: He solved the problem. Passive: The problem was solved by him. The options except (a) don't follow the correct grammatical format of the Passive Voice. Therefore, the correct answer is: (a).

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. The company has launched the product.
options: The product was launched by the company.
The product is launched by the company.
The product has been launched by the company.
The company has been launched by the product.
questions.explanation: The subject of the Active Voice is changed into the object of the Passive Voice. Also, the object of the Active Voice is changed into the subject of the Passive Voice. The usage of a "to be" form of verb ["been" in this case] is necessary while changing the voice of the sentence from Active to Passive. In Passive Voice, the preposition "by" is used before the object. However, it is optional in some cases. The Active voice in this question is in the present perfect, indicated by the verb phrase "has launched". Thus, the Passive voice will also be in the present perfect. The format of the Active voice is: Subject + has/have + V3 + Object. Thus, in Passive voice, the format will be: Object of the active voice (Subjective case) + has/have + been + V3 + by + Subject of the active voice (Objective case). For example: Active: They have completed the task. Passive: The task has been completed by them. The correct answer is shown in option (c). Therefore, the correct answer is: (c).

questions.question: Select the sentence which gives the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Go the extra mile
options: The courier just dropped the package at the doorstep without even ringing the doorbell once.
The hotel staff not only cleaned our room but also left a personalized welcome note.
He finished his assigned work exactly on time but did not do anything more than required.
She felt that her significant contributions to the team project went completely unnoticed by her superiors.
questions.explanation: The idiom "go the extra mile" means to make a special effort to do more than what is expected or required. Option (a): This describes poor service, which is the opposite of the idiom. Incorrect. Option (b): The hotel staff did their job (cleaned the room) and then did something extra (left a personalized note), which perfectly illustrates the idiom. Correct. Option (c): This describes doing the bare minimum, not making an extra effort. Incorrect. Option (d): This is about effort not being recognized and doesn't explain the meaning of making an extra effort. Incorrect. Hence, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Like a lamb to the slaughter
options: To be calm and not complaining.
questions.explanation: If someone does something or goes somewhere like a lamb to the slaughter, they do it without knowing that something bad is going to happen and therefore act calmly and without fighting against the situation. Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Find the synonym of "Idle".
questions.explanation: The word "idle" means avoiding work or being inactive. (a) "Active" is the opposite of "idle". (b) "Colorful" relates to appearance. (c) "Lazy" means unwilling to work, matching "idle". (d) "Accurate" relates to correctness. Therefore, "lazy" is the correct synonym.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with most appropriate tense. At this time next year, she ____ in the same organisation for ten years.
questions.explanation: The phrase "at this time next year" fixes a future point, while "for ten years" indicates duration. Therefore, the future perfect continuous tense (will have been working) is required. Hence, (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with an appropriate option. The soup was quite _____ and needed more seasoning.
questions.explanation: The correct option to fill in the blank is "bland". 'Bland' means lacking strong flavour; tasteless or mild. In the given context, it indicates that the soup was quite tasteless and needed more seasoning. Other words do not fit the given context. 'Trivial' means of little value or importance. 'Abrupt' means sudden and unexpected. 'Superficial' means existing or occurring at or on the surface. Hence, (d) is the correct answer. The complete sentence is: The soup was quite bland and needed more seasoning.

questions.question: Choose the sentence that uses a homonym of the underlined word: The fine print in the contract was hard to read.
options: The artist created a fine painting with intricate details.
He paid the parking fine at the city hall.
The weather was fine for the outdoor concert.
She received a fine award for her contributions.
questions.explanation: In the original sentence, "fine" is an adjective meaning very small or detailed. Options a) and c) use "fine" in the same sense. Option b) uses "fine" as a monetary penalty, which is unrelated to quality. Hence, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Which of the following sentences is grammatically correct?
options: This is the man who helped me.
This is the man who he helped me.
This is the man whom helped me.
This is the man he helped me.
questions.explanation: The correct sentence is (a). "Who" as a subject relative pronoun correctly introduces the clause without an extra pronoun. Option (b) is incorrect due to redundant "he" after "who," which already serves as the subject. Option (c) is incorrect due to "whom", which is an object relative pronoun and not required in this case. Option (d) is incorrect as it lacks a relative pronoun, creating a fragment. Hence, the correct answer is option (a). Note: Relative pronouns: A relative pronoun is used to connect a clause or phrase to a noun or pronoun. The clause modifies, or describes, the noun. The most common relative pronouns are who, whom, whose, which, and that. For example, she is the girl who received the award for bravery from our Home Minister. Subjective Pronouns: The pronouns I, you, we, he, she, it, and they are known as subjective pronouns because they act as the subjects of verbs: We went to the party. Objective Pronouns: The pronouns me, you, us, him, her, it, and them are called objective pronouns because they act as the objects of verbs and prepositions: Anjali drove us home.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. The soldiers, besides the general, has taken up arms against the king.
questions.explanation: The error lies in option (b). If two subjects are separated by 'besides', the verb agrees with the main subject. For example: The thief, besides his apprentice, has been caught by the police. Similarly, in this context, 'soldiers' is the main subject. As the subject is plural in form, the verb that must agree with it must also be plural. Hence, 'has' must be replaced by 'have'. Thus, the error is in option (b) the general has taken. Therefore, the corrected form of the sentence is: The soldiers, besides the general, have taken up arms against the king. Additional Information: If two subjects are separated by anything except "and", the verb agrees with the main subject. The list of such words is: As well as Like Unlike Besides In addition to With Together with Along with Including Accompanied by But

questions.question: In the following question, one part of the sentence has an error. Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error and choose the option accordingly. In any organisation, the most active and critical area is the computer support help desk.
questions.explanation: When two adjectives qualify the same noun, both adjectives should be expressed in the same degree. 'Most' is a superlative degree. Therefore, we should use 'most' before 'critical'. The correct sentence is: In any organisation, the most active and most critical area is the computer support help desk. Hence, option (a) is the correct answer. Note: Adjectives: Words which are used with a noun/pronoun to describe or point out, the person, animal, place, or thing which the noun names, or to tell the number or quantity, are called Adjectives. · The Positive Degree of an Adjective is the Adjective in its simple form. It is used to denote the mere existence of some quality of what we speak about. It is used when no comparison is made. · The Comparative degree of an adjective denotes a higher degree of the quality than the positive and is used when two things are compared. · The Superlative degree indicates the highest degree of the quality and is used when more than two things (or sets of things) are compared.

questions.question: Choose the antonym of "Courageous".
questions.explanation: The word "courageous" means showing bravery or fearlessness. (a) "Adventurous" means willing to take risks and is close in meaning to being brave. (b) "Cheerful" means happy or positive in mood and does not relate to bravery. (c) "Timid" means lacking courage or confidence and directly opposes bravery. (d) "Metallic" relates to metal and has no relation to human traits. Hence, "timid" is the correct antonym of "courageous", making option (c) the correct choice.

questions.question: Which sentence contains a word that is spelled incorrectly?
options: The proposal was approved without hesitation.
She displayed remarkable enthusiam for the project.
The instructions were clear and precise.
He handled the situation with maturity.
questions.explanation: Among the given options, (b) is incorrect because the word "enthusiam" is misspelt. The correct spelling is "enthusiasm", which means eager interest or excitement. Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. A man who is womanish in his habits.
questions.explanation: 'EFFEMINATE' means 'A man who is womanish in his habits'. 'CHRONOLOGY' means 'Arrangement of events in order of occurrence'. 'ALTRUIST' means 'A person who cares about others and helps them despite not gaining anything by doing this'. 'SIMPLETON' means 'A foolish or gullible person'. According to the meanings mentioned above, option a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Pick the spelling that is correct for a medical condition.
questions.explanation: The correct spelling is "Diabetes." It refers to a metabolic disease characterized by high blood sugar levels. The incorrect versions alter the vowel sounds or add extra letters, making them invalid. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Isolated
questions.explanation: Together - With or in proximity to another person or people Sociable - Willing to talk and engage in activities with other people; friendly. Solitary - Being, living, or going alone or without companions Common - Occurring, found, or done often; prevalent. The meaning of the word 'isolated' is 'having minimal contact or little in common with others'. Among the options, the word that is closely synonymous with the word 'isolated' is option (c) 'solitary'. Hence, the correct answer is option (c).

questions.question: What is driving the investment in renewable energy sources?
options: Need to combat climate change and reduce pollution
questions.explanation: As per the following sentence from paragraph 1, "Driven by the need to combat climate change and reduce air pollution, countries are investing heavily in these technologies," it can be noted that the need to counter attack climate change and pollution are the major drivers of investment in renewable energy sources. Hence, the correct answer is option (a).

questions.question: How do solar photovoltaic panels generate electricity?
questions.explanation: As per the following sentence from paragraph 1, "Solar photovoltaic panels convert sunlight into electricity, with costs plummeting due to manufacturing efficiencies and economies of scale," it can be noted that photovoltaic cells convert sunlight into electricity. Hence, the correct answer is option (a).

questions.question: What addresses the intermittency issues in renewables?
questions.explanation: As per the following sentence from paragraph 2, "Energy storage solutions, like lithium-ion batteries, address intermittency issues, ensuring reliable supply during non-generating periods," it can be noted that solutions like energy storage using lithium-ion batteries can resolve the intermittency issues in renewables. Hence, the correct answer is option (b).

questions.question: What benefit do off-grid solar systems provide in developing nations?
questions.explanation: As per the following sentence from paragraph 2, "In developing nations, off-grid solar systems empower rural communities with access to electricity, boosting education and economic opportunities," it can be noted that off-grid solar systems provide empowerment to rural communities with access to electricity thereby encouraging education and economic opportunities. Hence, the correct answer is option (b).

questions.question: What is crucial for achieving a net-zero emissions future?
questions.explanation: As per the following sentence from paragraph 3, "Ultimately, achieving a net-zero emissions future hinges on scaling renewables globally, fostering energy independence, and minimizing geopolitical tensions over oil reserves," to achieve a net-zero emissions future, it is essential to scale global renewable energy thereby fostering energy independence. Hence, the correct answer is option (a).

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active voice. The task must be finished by you today.
options: You must finish the task today.
You must be finishing the task today.
The task must finish you today.
You must have finished the task today.
questions.explanation: The subject of the Passive Voice is changed into the object of the Active Voice. Also, the object of the Passive Voice is changed into the subject of the Active Voice. The removal of the "to be" verb ['be'] and the preposition "by" is necessary while changing the voice of the sentence from Passive to Active. The Passive voice in this question contains a Modal verb (must). In Passive voice, the format is: Object of the Active voice (Subjective case) + Modal + be + V3 + by + Subject of the Active voice (Objective case). Thus, the format of Active voice is: Subject + Modal + V1 + Object. For example: This can be done by you. (Passive Voice) You can do this. (Active Voice) The options except (a) don't follow the correct grammatical format of the Active Voice. Therefore, the correct answer is option (a): You must finish the task today. Note: V1 -> Base form/simple form of verb, V2 -> Past form of verb (-ed form or irregular verb), V3 -> Past participle form of verb (-ed, -d, -t, -en, or -n), V4 -> Continuous form of verb / Present participle form of verb (-ing form), V5 -> Third person singular (V1+ -es or -s).

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in reported speech. The new fellow said, "What is the emergency contact number?"
options: The new fellow had been asked what the emergency contact number was.
The new fellow asked what the emergency contact number is been.
The new fellow asked for what the emergency contact number was being.
The new fellow asked what the emergency contact number was.
questions.explanation: In the case of Interrogative sentences like this, "said" in Direct Speech is changed into "enquired (of)", "wanted to know", "asked", etc. In Indirect speech, the verb is always placed after the subject, unlike in Direct Speech. For example: The teacher said to him, "What is your name?" (Direct Speech) The teacher asked him what his name was. (Indirect Speech) Also, the sentence in the Direct Speech is in the present indefinite tense. So, the tense in the Indirect Speech should be in the past indefinite tense. The following things in the sentence should change when changed from Direct speech to Indirect Speech: · The verb 'Is' changes to 'was'. · The reporting verb 'said' changes to 'asked'. Therefore, the correct answer is: (d)
